key_insight: EquiFiLM adds charge or other external conditioning to equivariant foundation force fields with a simple FiLM block on scalar channels, enabling accurate, low-data modeling of driven electronic-state changes without breaking E(3) symmetry.
engineering_relevance: This makes pretrained ML force fields usable for charged, excited, or field-driven simulations in catalysis, electrochemistry, and ultrafast dynamics at near-DFT fidelity and with negligible extra inference cost.
method: Insert per-layer FiLM modulation into an equivariant backbone, conditioning only scalar features so symmetry is preserved exactly.
Train on a small set of DFT-labeled frames for the target external condition, then interpolate and extrapolate across unseen condition values.
Demonstrate the recipe on charged liquid water using MACE-MatPES as the backbone, producing E-MACE.
evidence_signal: On four training charges, E-MACE reduced force RMSE from 21.3 to 6.96 meV/Å and energy RMSE from 6.1 to 0.1 meV/atom, and remained stable across seven held-out charges with force RMSE of 18–61 meV/Å.
watch_item: Performance still depends on having representative labeled data across the conditioning range, and extrapolation beyond the tested charge domain may be less reliable.
paper_title: EquiFiLM: Charge-Conditioned Equivariant Force Fields via Feature-wise Linear Modulation
